seo.title: RTK Query
seo.description: RTK Query data fetching patterns: semantic data renaming, loading states, error handling with toast notifications

header.title: Data Management via RTK Query
header.description: Detailed guide on RTK Query architecture (Code Splitting).

invariant_text: It is forbidden to use <one>createApi</one> inside business modules of entities. The project uses a single caching core, divided into configuration and authorization layers. Features and entities "inject" their endpoints into the authorized core.

tags.title: Global Tag Registry (Shared Layer)
tags.description: For correct operation of invalidation <one>providesTags / invalidatesTags</one>, all possible tags are collected in a centralized config.

base.title: Base API Instance (Shared Layer)
base.description: The lowest level of network requests. No interceptors, only pure <one>fetchBaseQuery</one> and global tag registration.

auth_query.title: Authorization Interceptor (Entities Layer)
auth_query.description: A wrapper (Middleware) over the base request. It allows centralized handling of session loss (e.g., error 401) and executing <one>logout()</one> for the entire application.

auth_api.title: Authorized API Instance (Entities Layer)
auth_api.description: Initialization of a protected API instance (usually exported from the <one>auth</one> entity) using a custom <two>authBaseQuery</two>.

inject.title: Endpoint Injection (Entities Layer)
inject.description: The final stage in the target entity. We import <one>authApi</one> and call <two>injectEndpoints</two>. This code becomes the public service of the entity.
